etymology.description: 胳(胳) (gē, "arm, upper arm") originally referred to the fleshy part of the arm, drawing on the imagery of meat/flesh (月, radical for body parts), combining with a phonetic component 合(hé, "to combine") for its sound. 膊(膊) (bó, "shoulder, arm") focuses on the shoulder area, also using the flesh radical (月), paired with the phonetic component 铂(bó, "platinum") providing its reading.
etymology.interpretation: The combination of 胳(胳) (gē, "upper arm") and 膊(膊) (bó, "shoulder") represents the entire upper limb. 胳 suggests the fleshy part of the arm, while 膊 emphasizes the shoulder's relation to movement/strength - collectively encompassing the 'arm'.

examples.chinese: 他的胳膊受伤了(他的胳膊受傷了)。
小女孩的胳膊很长(小女孩的胳膊很長)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo nǚhái de gēbo hěn cháng.
examples.english: The little girl's arms are long.

examples.chinese: 我举起胳膊(我舉起胳膊)，挥了挥手(揮了揮手)。
examples.english: I raised my arm and waved.
